title: Hybrid Retrieval with BM25 and Dense Embeddings

questions.question: Why does a production RAG endpoint need both lexical and semantic retrieval at once?
options: Semantic retrieval is always more accurate
Lexical search is required by law for compliance
The query distribution mixes literal-identifier and paraphrased queries; one modality fails on each class
It halves the index size
questions.explanation: BM25 dominates on literal symbols; dense dominates on paraphrases. A real endpoint receives both classes from the same users.

questions.question: What is the BM25 parameter b at the default 0.75?
options: A multiplier on the IDF term
A length-normalization knob: longer documents are penalized but not linearly
A query-time exponent on cosine similarity
The number of fields in the document
questions.explanation: b interpolates between no length normalization (0) and full normalization (1); 0.75 is the published recommendation.

questions.question: What does Reciprocal Rank Fusion compute for each candidate?
options: Sum of 1 / (k + rank) across each ranked list that contains the candidate
Product of cosine similarities across modalities
Average of the BM25 and dense scores after min-max normalization
The median rank across modalities
questions.explanation: RRF sums 1 / (k + rank) contributions per ranked list; k = 60 is the published default.

questions.question: Why is rank-based fusion preferred over a linear interpolation of BM25 and cosine scores?
options: Cosine scores are unbounded
BM25 scores cannot be sorted
BM25 scores are unbounded and corpus-dependent; cosine is bounded in -1 to 1; the score scales are not comparable across corpora
Linear interpolation requires GPU support
questions.explanation: Score interpolation breaks every reindex because the score distributions shift; ranks are comparable across modalities by construction.

questions.question: What happens to RRF when you make the constant k very small?
options: The top-1 contribution dominates the fusion; deep ranks contribute almost nothing
The fusion becomes equivalent to a linear score sum
Out-of-corpus terms get a positive score
The fusion ignores the second modality
questions.explanation: Small k concentrates the rank-1 contribution; large k flattens the contribution curve and lets deep candidates vote.

questions.question: Which failure mode does field weighting through token repetition specifically avoid?
options: Out-of-vocabulary embeddings
Adding a second score-aggregation step at query time; the math stays identical and per-field-score combination logic is not needed
BM25 IDF collapsing to zero
Cosine similarity going negative
questions.explanation: Repeating tokens at index time multiplies term frequency, which lets the BM25 scoring math stay one summation rather than a separate field-score combinator.
